text: As I stand here, the world feels clinical and curated. The glass walls separate me from an ordered space, while bright colors and bold letters announce the arena of research and development. I am part of an experiment, or at least, a participant in an unfolding inquiry. The hum of fluorescent lights and the sharp clarity of text and screens make me feel exposed, a bit on display, pondering if the polished surface between observer and observed signifies connection or separation. I sense my own presence as both a witness and an object within this deliberate arrangement.
